evidences.title: Brazil Say Neymar Making Good Progress On Injury
evidences.content: Neymar has received a positive injury update as Brazil wait to learn whether he can return during the 2026 World Cup group stage. Neymar has received a positive injury update as Brazil wait to learn whether he can return during the 2026 World Cup group stage (Photo by Wagner Meier/Getty Images) Neymar’s chances of playing a role at the 2026 FIFA World Cup have improved after the Brazilian Football Confederation said the forward is progressing as expected in his recovery from a calf injury. The 34-year-old underwent another MRI scan on Monday after previously being diagnosed with a Grade 2 muscle injury in his right calf. Brazil’s medical staff said the latest test showed encouraging signs, although no return date has been confirmed. [...] For Brazil supporters and those tracking soccer betting sites during the tournament, Neymar’s availability remains one of the key storylines around Group C. The latest medical update is positive, but Brazil are still waiting to see whether their record scorer can return in time to affect the opening phase of the World Cup. ## Brazil World Cup 2026 Fixtures | Date | Opponent | Venue | Time (ET) | --- --- | | Sat, June 13 | Morocco | MetLife Stad...

Infringements concerning the eligibility of players shall be decided by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee in accordance with the FIFA Disciplinary Code. evidences.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: International eligibility rules explained
evidences.content: They must be fielded, not simply be listed as a substitute. Once they are "cap-tied" as soccer fans call it, there are limited ways in which they may change. Generally, only players who see time in friendly matches, which are non-official competitions, are able to switch to another nationality which they are eligible for after playing in a match. Article 9 of FIFA's regulations state: "A player may, only once, request to change the association for which heis eligible to play to the association of another country of which he holds the nationality." [...] There are additional caveats that indicate whether or not the request to change will be approved. For instance, FIFA regulations indicate that switch will be granted only if the player held the nationality he is switching to at the time of being fielded for the other team. Players who have played more than three matches for another team will not be granted the switch. Once a player changes, they are locked into that team - hence the name "one-time change of association," sometimes called a "one-time switch." Players at the World Cup who fall into this category include Australia's Cristian Volpato, who switched from Italy, injured...

evidences.content: Sources told ESPN Brasil that Neymar was accompanied to the clinic by the head of Brazil's medical staff Rodrigo Lasmar where he underwent an MRI scan. Neymar had reported to camp earlier Wednesday, joining the rest of the squad at the five-time world champions' training center in Teresópolis, outside Rio de Janeiro. Pending the results of the final medical tests, it appears highly unlikely that Neymar will feature in Brazil's first warm-up friendly ahead of the World Cup this coming Sunday against Panama in Brasília. Brazil's second warmup match is against Egypt on June 5. [...] ## ESPN # Neymar misses first Brazil training camp after further tests Copy Link Neymar missed the first World Cup training session for Brazil on Wednesday to undergo "further medical evaluations" on his bruised right calf, the Brazilian Football Confederation (CBF) announced. evidences.title: List of international goals scored by Neymar - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Neymar's first senior international tournament was the 2011 Copa América, where he scored two goals for Brazil. He played in his first senior FIFA tournament two years later, when he starred for hosts Brazil in the 2013 FIFA Confederations Cup. Neymar scored four goals throughout the campaign, including in the final against Spain, helping Brazil to a 3–0 victory. Neymar received the Bronze Shoe for his four goals, and was awarded with the Golden Ball for his standout performances throughout the tournament. Neymar played at his first FIFA World Cup in 2014, where he scored four goals, placing himself as the joint-third top scorer of the competition alongside his club teammate Lionel Messi and Dutch forward Robin van Persie. He also scored twice during both the 2018 and 2022 World Cup. evidences.title: FIFA eligibility rules - Wikipedia
evidences.content: > Any player who refers to art. 5 par. 1 to assume a new nationality and who has not played international football [in a match (either in full or in part) in an official competition of any category or any type of football] shall be eligible to play for the new representative team only if he fulfils one of the following conditions: > a) He was born on the territory of the relevant association; > b) His biological mother or biological father was born on the territory of the relevant association; > c) His grandmother or grandfather was born on the territory of the relevant association; > d) He has lived continuously for at least five years after reaching the age of 18 on the territory of the relevant association. — FIFA.com [...] Rules to determine player participation In association football, the FIFA eligibility rules are the eligibility criteria established by FIFA (International Association Football Federation), the sport's governing body, to facilitate the selection of representative teams for international competitions.
